dailyJudgementSnippet: Google Gemini won the day because Deep Think generated the strongest favorable HN reaction of any tracked provider, with commenters highlighting benchmark gains, practical low-cost API use, and a sense that Google had pulled ahead; its other direct mention was more cautious than euphoric, but not enough to offset that momentum [E1][E2].
winnerExplanation: Google Gemini stood out on both intensity and breadth of positive discussion. The Deep Think launch thread produced clear excitement around reported performance and real-world utility, giving Gemini the day's most convincing positive driver, while the separate legal-reasoning thread kept Gemini visible without turning sharply negative [E1][E2].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex Spark drew favorable interest for speed and agent workflow usefulness, but users also described it as having a smaller-model feel, and the legal-reasoning paper was heavily qualified by commenters who said it mostly measured rigid formalism rather than real judicial judgment [E3][E4].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Anthropic's funding round and stated revenue run rate were treated as notable, but the comments quickly turned to mission drift, capital-intensity skepticism, and whether challengers can keep up with Google; separately, a benchmark comparison in the Gemini thread explicitly framed Opus 4.6 as well behind the new Google model [E5][E6].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Deep Think produced the strongest positive reaction among the tracked providers, driven by benchmark excitement, comments that Google was 'running away with it,' and a concrete report of strong transcription results at low API cost; the legal-reasoning paper was discussed more cautiously, but still reflected Gemini as a top-performing model in the comparison [E1][E2].

evidence.summary: In the Gemini 3 Deep Think thread, top comments emphasized the reported ARC-AGI-2 score, praised Google as pulling ahead, and shared a practical Gemini API use case with strong results and low cost.

The legal-reasoning story directly named Gemini 3 Pro as following the law 'without fail,' but commenters argued the paper overstated what that meant and framed it as formalism rather than true judgment.
The GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark launch thread was broadly favorable about speed and usefulness, with one detailed user report calling it blazing fast, while also noting tradeoffs versus larger Codex models.

The legal-reasoning story centered on GPT-5 outperforming judges on the paper's metric, but top comments pushed back that the result mostly captured strict rule-following and that the headline overstated the implication.

Anthropic's $30B funding round prompted recognition of its rapid revenue growth, but the discussion was mixed, with skepticism about private-company scale, capital demands, and how sustainable the competitive race is.

In the Gemini 3 Deep Think thread, a prominent benchmark comparison highlighted Gemini at 84.6% versus Opus 4.6 at 68.8%, creating a direct head-to-head comment that reflected poorly on Anthropic's model.
